content:
MEMORANDUM

TO: [Attorney Name]

FROM: [Your Name]

DATE: [Today's Date]

RE: Internal Case Summary for Keystone Building Group, Inc. - Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building Project

Project Overview

Project Name: Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building

Location: 4850 Military Trail, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33418

Owner: Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC

General Contractor: Keystone Building Group, Inc.

Key Subcontractors: Information not explicitly available in provided documents

Contract Amount: $24,750,000.00

Contract Type: Standard Form of Agreement (specific form type not provided)

Project Scope: Construction of a medical office building, including structural, mechanical, and electrical elements as identified in project documents.

Dispute Overview

The dispute centers on delay claims related to the Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building project. Keystone Building Group, Inc., claims delays due to late responses to RFIs, owner-directed design changes, and delayed delivery of owner-furnished equipment. Keystone seeks a contract extension of 94 days and additional compensation totaling $1,847,000.00. The Owner, Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC, has denied the claim, citing untimeliness and contractual defenses, including no damages for delay except in specific circumstances.

Parties

Owner: Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC

General Contractor: Keystone Building Group, Inc.

Counsel for Owner: Not identified in provided documents

Counsel for Contractor: Not identified in provided documents

Relevant Contract Terms

Notice Requirements: Contractor must notify the Owner and Architect in writing of claims for additional time or compensation within 14 days of the event (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Time Extension Procedures: Not detailed beyond initial notice requirements. Specific procedures for submission and handling are missing (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Dispute Resolution Clauses: Covered in the contract but specifics are not quoted or detailed (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Liquidated Damages: $4,500 per calendar day for delays beyond substantial completion, capped at $600,000 (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

Retainage Provisions: Not explicitly detailed in the provided documents.

Chronology

April 12, 2023: Contract executed (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

May 1, 2023: Notice to proceed issued (01_Prime_Contract_Extract.txt).

October 17, 2023: RFI-089 issued regarding a structural conflict (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

November 14, 2023: Delayed response to RFI-089 received (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

February 29, 2024: Schedule Update No. 11 indicates project is 94 days behind (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

March 22, 2024: Notice of Claim letter by Keystone dated (04_Notice_of_Claim_Letter.txt).

April 15, 2024: Owner's Denial Letter issued (05_Owner_Denial_Letter.txt).

Document Gaps

While the core allegations and defenses are detailed in the available documents, there are significant gaps including:

- Specific contractual references that might support Keystone's claims for a contract extension and additional compensation (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

- Delivery dates for owner-furnished equipment are not confirmed (03_Schedule_Update_11_Narrative.txt).

- Detailed impact analysis for cost and time related to project delays (02_RFI_089_and_Log.txt).

- Justifications for the $1,847,000 claim amount (04_Notice_of_Claim_Letter.txt).

```html

Bayshore Tower Condominium Association

Defect Summary Memorandum

Category: Waterproofing Defects

Project Location: Bayshore Tower Condominium, Balconies and Balcony-to-Wall Transitions

Condition Description: The waterproofing membrane installed on the balconies did not comply with the manufacturer's instructions, requiring removal and reinstallation due to inadequate application procedures. The conflict exists between the architectural detail and manufacturer's requirements for the balcony-to-wall transition, specifically regarding the upturn height and termination exposure.

Date of Discovery/Documentation: Initial installation issue identified by the superintendent on September 28, 2022. RFI regarding detail conflict issued on June 3, 2022, with an architect's response on June 13, 2022.

Responsible Party: Meridian Construction Group, LLC, with specific responsibility noted to the Senior Superintendent, Raymond Castillo, who recognized the issue in August 2022.

Documents Evidencing the Defect:

  • Change Order 011 - Additional waterproofing work details
  • Deposition of Raymond Castillo - Acknowledgment of premature application and defect documentation issues
  • Daily Log dated August 12, 2022 - Application of membrane before concrete curing
  • RFI 047 Waterproofing Detail - Conflict in installation requirements

Testing Findings, Expert Conclusions, or Repair Estimates: Change Order details the additional costs of $86,930 for the compliance modification work. No specific testing results or expert conclusions provided in the documents.

Code Compliance Issues: The need to follow the manufacturer's requirement for at least 28-day curing or 75% design strength of the concrete substrate prior to membrane application reflects compliance concerns.

Document Support: Change Order 011, Deposition of Raymond Castillo, Daily Log 2022-08-12, RFI 047 Waterproofing Detail

Additional Notes: Discrepancies were noted in the witness deposition regarding awareness and documentation timelines, reflecting inconsistencies in defect reporting procedures.

```
